name: Housing Action Illinois
description_short: Illinois nonprofit that mobilizes member organizations to prevent homelessness, increase affordable housing, and expand homeownership through policy advocacy, counseling, and capacity building.
description_long: Housing Action Illinois is a statewide nonprofit organization that has operated for nearly 40 years as a coalition builder and advocacy organization focused on housing security and affordability across Illinois. The organization does not provide direct consumer services but rather mobilizes a network of member organizations—including housing counseling agencies, homeless service providers, affordable housing developers, and policymakers—to advance shared housing goals. Their work encompasses policy advocacy to change housing laws and regulations, education and organizing to shift narratives around housing issues, and capacity building support for member organizations to expand their resources and reach. Housing Action Illinois operates as a HUD-approved Intermediary, manages an AmeriCorps network that places volunteers with community organizations, and runs a Housing Counseling Training Center that certifies housing counselors. The organization has documented impact including tens of thousands of new homes built, hundreds of thousands of people transitioned from homelessness or housing insecurity to stable housing, and millions of renters gaining better legal protections. What distinguishes Housing Action Illinois is its focus on systemic change and member support rather than direct consumer counseling—they build capacity in the housing counseling field rather than counsel individuals directly. They also provide a "Critical Connections for Housing Counseling" referral service that connects consumers to HUD-approved housing counselors in their network without confusing forms or lengthy searches. An honest assessment notes that this organization is best suited for those seeking to connect with trained housing counselors through their network, policymakers interested in housing advocacy, nonprofit organizations seeking capacity building, or AmeriCorps members seeking placement opportunities. Those seeking immediate individual housing counseling should be aware that Housing Action Illinois functions as a coordinator and trainer of counselors rather than a direct service provider.
pros: HUD-approved Intermediary status ensures rigorous standards for housing counseling services in their network
40-year track record with documented impact: tens of thousands of homes built and hundreds of thousands housed
Manages AmeriCorps network, providing volunteer support to community organizations addressing local housing challenges
Operates Housing Counseling Training Center to certify and build capacity of housing counselors throughout Illinois
Free, simple referral service (Critical Connections) connecting consumers to HUD-approved counselors without confusing applications
Comprehensive policy advocacy covering homelessness, affordable rental housing, sustainable homeownership, and housing discrimination
Member-based coalition model creates statewide coordination across housing counseling agencies, developers, and service providers
cons: Does not provide direct housing counseling—refers consumers to member organizations, adding an intermediary step
Primarily advocacy and capacity-building focused rather than consumer-facing services, limiting direct support availability
As a coalition organization, service quality and responsiveness may vary depending on member agency capabilities
Website does not clearly specify response times or availability for housing counselor referrals
Limited direct assistance for consumers needing immediate individual housing counseling without referral navigation
best_for: Illinois residents seeking connections to HUD-approved housing counselors without navigating confusing application processes
Nonprofit organizations and housing agencies seeking capacity building, training, and AmeriCorps volunteer support
Policymakers and advocates interested in statewide housing policy changes and data on Illinois housing affordability
Housing counselors and aspiring counselors seeking professional training, certification, and ongoing professional development
diagnosis: Housing Action Illinois is best for Illinois residents seeking to connect with trained HUD-approved housing counselors through their free referral service, and for nonprofit organizations looking to build capacity and expand housing services through training and AmeriCorps partnerships. The main caveat is that this organization functions as a coordinator and advocacy body rather than a direct service provider—consumers needing individual housing counseling will be referred to member agencies rather than counseled directly by Housing Action Illinois staff.
services: Housing counselor referrals through Critical Connections service
HUD-approved housing counselor training and certification
Policy advocacy for homelessness prevention and affordable housing
Capacity building for housing nonprofits and counseling agencies
AmeriCorps network management and volunteer placement
Education and organizing around housing discrimination and civil rights
Annual benefit event for supporter engagement
Research and publications on Illinois housing issues
Trainings and events for housing nonprofits and counselors
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 67 E. Madison Street, Suite 1603, , Chicago, IL 60603
meta_description: Illinois nonprofit mobilizing organizations to prevent homelessness, increase affordable housing, and expand homeownership through advocacy and counseling.
